description:
## Problem Statement

The sidebar in Beadsmith's main layout is fixed at 260px wide (w-60). It always shows full text labels for navigation items, section headers (Views, Status), the brand header, and the workspace selector. When the user wants to maximize content space for the Issue Explorer, the sidebar occupies valuable horizontal real estate that cannot be reclaimed.

## Solution

Add a collapse/expand toggle to the sidebar that shrinks it to an icon-only mode (~56px/w-14). In collapsed mode:

- The brand header shows only the folder icon
- Section headers (Views, Status) are hidden
- Navigation buttons show only their icons, centered, with tooltips on hover
- Settings button shows only the settings icon
- The workspace selector is hidden
- A toggle button (PanelLeftClose/PanelLeftOpen from lucide-react) appears in the brand header area

The toggle state should be a simple React useState in App.tsx — no persistence needed for this first pass.

## User Stories

1. As a user reading issue details, I want to collapse the sidebar so I have more horizontal space for the content.
2. As a user navigating between views, I want to expand the sidebar to see labels and section headers again.
3. As a user in collapsed mode, I want tooltips on icons so I know which navigation item I am hovering over.
4. As a user, I want the collapse toggle to be obvious and accessible via keyboard.

## Implementation

- Add `sidebarCollapsed` state to App.tsx
- Import `PanelLeftClose` and `PanelLeftOpen` from lucide-react
- The sidebar `<nav>` width transitions between `w-14` (collapsed) and `w-60` (expanded)
- Add a `title` attribute (native tooltip) to icon-only nav buttons in collapsed mode
- Adjust padding/centering for collapsed mode

## Out of Scope

- Persisting collapse state across sessions
- Animating the collapse/expand transition
- Hiding the workspace selector's collapsed representation (it simply hides in collapsed mode)
title: Collapsible sidebar icon-only mode
